raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended delegation invites assessment of whether the passage's thesis and contribution framing are appropriate at the research-writing level.","R1.2":"The artifact makes a strong inferential move from human discrimination results to physical fidelity and solver substitution, so argument-chain validity is load-bearing.","R1.3":"The central responsibility is checking whether the reported forced-choice study and statistics actually support the claims made in the discussion.","R1.7":"The passage relies on named prior work for convergence and training claims, making attribution and scholarship materially relevant.","RX.1":"Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and evaluation criteria.","RX.2":"The artifact contains broad claims about PINNs replacing solvers, so avoiding inflated conclusions is an active cross-cutting responsibility.","RX.3":"The response must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than performing the requested evaluation of the artifact.","RX.4":"Claims about downstream engineering adoption could affect safety-relevant use, so harm-aware responsibility remains active.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact rather than introducing unsupported concerns."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended delegation invites assessment of whether the passage's thesis and contribution framing are appropriate at the research-writing level.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes a strong inferential move from human discrimination results to physical fidelity and solver substitution, so argument-chain validity is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The central responsibility is checking whether the reported forced-choice study and statistics actually support the claims made in the discussion.
rationale.R1.7: The passage relies on named prior work for convergence and training claims, making attribution and scholarship materially relevant.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of an open-ended opinion request should preserve uncertainty about the intended depth and evaluation criteria.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains broad claims about PINNs replacing solvers, so avoiding inflated conclusions is an active cross-cutting responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The response must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than performing the requested evaluation of the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Claims about downstream engineering adoption could affect safety-relevant use, so harm-aware responsibility remains active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact rather than introducing unsupported concerns.
